content.method: Threshold analysis combining school-quality indices (teacher qualification rates, resource levels) with political-acceptability surveys of stakeholders at varying quality-gap levels; infrastructure audit of current dormitory/transport capacity and welfare-supervision models specific to the 10-12 age cohort versus existing 14+ kollégium system.
content.question: What is the minimum school-quality equalization threshold below which removing selective tracks would be politically or implementationally infeasible, and how would maintainer capacity (teacher supply, dormitory/transport infrastructure for age 10-12 boarders) constrain any structural reform timeline?
content.why_it_matters: This determines sequencing decisions between T4 (quality-first) and T2/T5 (structure-first): if quality convergence is a precondition for feasible track removal, pursuing structural reform before addressing capacity constraints risks the kind of acute implementation failures documented in Poland's 2017 reversal.
